system.background:
Belgae Tribe (will become part of Salisbury)

Father: Sir Geraint ap Llwyd [GEH-rynt ahp THLOOEED]

440: Constantine III murdered; Irish extend holdings. Fought at the Battle of the River Parrot against the Irish. Lost his right eye but gained an impressive, distinctive scar. +60 Glory. Developed a mild Suspicion of Atrebates warriors (Passion 7).

442: Both Gorthyn's father and Paun's father attended the coronation of Constans.

443: Constans is murdered.

446: Pict invasion. Fought at the Battle of Lincoln. Distinguished himself despite his dead right eye. +540 Glory..

447: Vortigern becomes High King. Saxons are given property; the Cymri were not consulted, many driven from their lands. A prelate from the Pope arrives to condemn laxities. Sir Geraint was present when the prelate healed someone of leprosy. +20 Glory.

448: More Saxons arrive, as does Rowena. When Cymrics complained about favoritism towards the Saxons, Sir Geraint kept his head down.

449: New foe on the continent: the Huns. Saxons prove useful defense against the Irish, pirates, and Picts. Sir Geraint kept his own counsel despite things getting worse.

450: Vortigern marries Rowena, gives her father Hengest the entire county of Kent. While hunting in the woodlands, Sir Geraint offended the fae, who cursed our family's hunting (-5 to Hunting rolls).

454: Fought the Irish in Cornwall. +920 Glory.

456: Switched allegiance to the dissidents. Fell ill.

457: Fought at the Battle of Kent on the side of the rebels. Died in battle. +60 Glory (Final Total: 2,282). Developed Hate (Vortigern) 13.

**Family Legacy (After Sir Geraint's Death)**

The family remains rebels.

458: Paun's uncle takes stewardship of the lands. Parts of the extended family pack up and move to Brittany.

461: Cousin fought at the Battle of Watsom Channel.

462: Relatives fought at the Battle of Ebbsfleet. Survived.

463: Uncle killed by Kent Saxons at the Night of the Long Knives.

**Paun's Inheritance:**

Hate (Saxons) Passion: rolled 15

Fae Curse: Hunting -5

Starting Glory: 570

system.description: Paun played harp and sang, and then spoke to one of the knights about falconry (he betrayed his ignorance). Then Branwen asked Paun to dance, and he was so flummoxed that he kept stepping on her toes and apologizing, and she teased him about it.

system.disadvantage: Always breaks on a fumble result or a tie against another sword.
